title: JTBD — NAICS 221122 Electric Power Distribution (DSO view, hardware tier)

sections.title: Phase 1 — Industry Landscape (reused from VN)
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| CFJ | Deliver bulk electrical energy from transmission interface to end-use premises at regulated voltage, frequency, reliability, and price |
| Offering level | L7 ecosystem (service, no BOM) |
| Output types | Delivered kWh (MV/LV), connection services, flexibility/congestion relief, metering data |

### Segments (DSO operator beneficiaries — by circumstance)

| Segment | Circumstance | Serves |
|---|---|---|
| S1 Urban high-density feeders | Load-growth-constrained, short reinforcement cycles | Cities, industrial zones |
| S2 Rural radial feeders | Long feeders, voltage drop, DG backflow | Agriculture, Dorfnetze |
| S3 DG-saturated feeders | >100% hosting capacity, curtailment under §14a | PV-dense regions (Bayern, BW) |
| S4 HV/MV interface stations | TSO-DSO handoff, redispatch pass-through | Grid-booster corridors |
| S5 Reinforcement-deferral candidates | 5–15 yr planning horizon, CAPEX-gated | DSOs under EnWG anreizregulierung |
| S6 N-1 weak spots | Single-fault exposure, SAIDI-driven | Rural + industrial sensitive |

**Immediate-customer segments (for planning):** DSO grid-planning dept, asset mgmt, grid control room, procurement (framework-contract).

VN / BOM / Edges: see `VN_electric_power_distribution_ibisworld_ht.md` Parts B–D.

---

sections.title: Phase 2 — Company Enters
sections.content:
### 2.1 VN Anchor

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Route | **PROCESS_VN** (BESS makes the CFJ happen; not inside delivered kWh) |
| Anchor level | L5 |
| Anchor unit | Grid-Scale Battery Storage (Netzbooster / Feeder-Relief BESS) |
| Parent unit | L6f Flexibility & Congestion Management |
| Parent-defined job context | Dispatch flexibility/storage to relieve MV/LV constraints |

**Alternative structure:** B2B deep — alternatives are other technologies at the **same L5 slot** (within L6f). Competition is slot-bound.

### 2.2 Lens

| Element | Value |
|---|---|
| Function verb (from L6f job_family) | Dispatch flexibility/storage to relieve constraints |
| Beneficiary | DSO grid-control + planning functions (acting for end-use premises) |
| Lens statement | "What dispatchable-flexibility capability does the DSO need to relieve feeder/interface constraints without deferring reinforcement beyond regulated reliability?" |

### 2.3 Core Job + Product Job

**Core Job (solution-agnostic, L6f-level):**
> "Relieve a defined MV/LV grid constraint on demand, without violating voltage/frequency/reliability obligations or exceeding the reinforcement-deferral cost ceiling."

**Product Job (anchored at L5 BESS):**
> "Help the DSO Flexibility & Congestion Management function to inject or absorb multi-MW real and reactive power at a specified grid node, on sub-second notice, under VDE-AR-N 4110 compliance, for a contracted lifetime and availability."

Abstraction test: ≥3 cross-category alternatives (BESS, STATCOM, copper reinforcement, DR/VPP, curtailment) → **PASS**.

### 2.4 Alternatives (5 retained)

| # | Alternative | UNSPSC / group | Inherent trade-offs | Existential? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | **Containerized BESS EPC (incumbent — Fluence / Tesla / Sungrow / Mein Solar class)** | custom: BESS EPC | Li-ion fire risk, 10-yr cal life, €250–400/kWh, 6–12 mo cert lead | — |
| A2 | STATCOM / SVC / mechanical FACTS | 39121611 Power conditioners | No energy storage (reactive only); can't defer kWh reinforcement | — |
| A3 | VPP / DR aggregation (distributed flex) | 81112005 Network services | Unreliable availability, depends on 3rd-party load response, no grid-forming | — |
| A4 | **Physical copper reinforcement (substation upgrade / new feeder)** | 72141107 Electrical transmission const. | 3–7 yr lead, €-intensive, permit-blocked, but permanent | **EXISTENTIAL** |
| A5 | Do-nothing / accept curtailment + redispatch | — | Zero CAPEX; mounting §13/§14a cost, AEE compensation exposure, reputational | — |

"Do nothing" carries zero P1 burden — status-quo incumbent for most DSOs under EnWG anreizregulierung incentives.

### 2.5 Scenarios (activate output types)

| Scenario | BOM/output activated | Dominant alt. |
|---|---|---|
| Sc1 Feeder-level congestion (MV primary) | MV feeder relief, §14a curtailment defer | A1 BESS, A4 copper |
| Sc2 HV/MV interface bottleneck (substation) | Substation deferral, grid-booster corridor | A1 BESS, A4 copper |
| Sc3 LV voltage-band violation (DG-dense) | LV voltage support, reactive power | A1 BESS, A2 STATCOM |
| Sc4 New-connection / hosting-capacity block | Admit IPP/load without reinforcement | A1 BESS, A4 copper |
| Sc5 Renewable curtailment recovery | Curtailed kWh capture | A1 BESS, A3 VPP |

---

sections.title: Phase 3 — Needs Derivation
sections.content:
### 3.1 Stakeholder roles (6, Burleson/Ulwick)

| Role | DSO-side persona |
|---|---|
| Job Beneficiary | End-use premises (indirect) — voltage/frequency quality |
| Job Executor | Grid control room operator; flex-dispatch desk |
| Job Overseer | Head of grid planning; CTO; BNetzA (regulator) |
| Lifecycle Support | EPC installer, TÜV/FGH certifier, O&M tech, recycler |
| Purchase Executor | DSO procurement; framework-contract manager |
| Purchase Influencer | BNetzA, VDE FNN, consulting (DNV, Lazard), insurer |

### 3.2 Burleson Pyramid — relevant job steps

Relevant steps (4 of 8): **DEFINE, PREPARE, EXECUTE, MONITOR**. Target: 15–30 error statements. Planned: 22.

### 3.3 P1 Lifecycle × Alternative summary

| Stage | A1 BESS | A4 Copper reinforcement | A5 Do-nothing |
|---|---|---|---|
| Acquire | Spec + VDE cert + DSO tender | Permit + land + multi-yr plan | — |
| Prepare | Install, commission, certify (6–12 mo) | Civil + line build (3–7 yr) | — |
| Use | Dispatch daily, SoC cycle | Passive, none | — |
| Maintain | BMS/PCS/cert audit annual | Decadal inspection | — |
| Dispose | Cell recycle (EU Battery Reg.) | Decommission passive assets | — |

**Key insight:** A5 has zero P1 burden — permanent structural competitor. A4 has huge Acquire/Prepare burden (3–7yr, €€€) but zero Use burden. A1 inherits continuous Use/Maintain burden in exchange for fast Acquire/Prepare.

### 3.4 Error Statements (22 total — ODI-scored)

Legend: **I**=importance, **S**=satisfaction, **O**=opportunity=I+max(I−S,0), **PR**=product_related (true=technology-caused), **Cat**=impact category.

| # | Stage | Step | Role | Err type | Statement | I | S | O | Cat | PR | Alt |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | DEFINE | Define | Planner | ACCURACY | Minimize the likelihood of under-sizing BESS power/energy rating such that ≥5% of required annual constraint-hours go unserved | 9 | 4 | 14 | reliability | F | — |
| 2 | DEFINE | Define | Planner | ACCURACY | Increase the ability to map a specific MV feeder constraint to a ≥95%-accurate required kW/kWh dispatch profile over a 10-yr horizon | 9 | 3 | 15 | reliability | F | — |
| 3 | DEFINE | Define | Overseer | EFFORT | Minimize the time to produce a BNetzA/anreizregulierung-compliant business case linking reinforcement deferral to the storage asset (target <90 days) | 8 | 4 | 12 | time | F | — |
| 4 | PREPARE | Prepare | Purchase Exec | ROBUSTNESS | Minimize the likelihood of grid-code certification (VDE-AR-N 4110 Type B, FGW TR8) slipping the project critical path by >3 months | 9 | 3 | 15 | time | T (A1) | A1 |
| 5 | PREPARE | Prepare | Lifecycle | EFFORT | Minimize the EPC interface count (number of separate counterparties the DSO must manage) to ≤1 for the storage asset | 8 | 4 | 12 | cost | F | — |
| 6 | PREPARE | Prepare | Purchase Exec | ACCURACY | Minimize the likelihood of fire-protection/Baugenehmigung rejection post-order causing >€200k rework | 9 | 5 | 13 | safety | T (A1) | A1 |
| 7 | PREPARE | Prepare | Planner | CONSISTENCY | Minimize variability in commissioning duration across sites with similar MV profiles (target σ <10 days across ≥5 sites) | 6 | 4 | 8 | time | F | — |
| 8 | EXECUTE | Execute | Executor | THROUGHPUT | Maximize contracted-availability hours per year delivered to the DSO dispatch signal (target ≥98% availability, ≥6,000 full-cycle equivalents over 10 yr) | 10 | 5 | 15 | reliability | F | — |
| 9 | EXECUTE | Execute | Executor | ACCURACY | Minimize dispatch setpoint-to-response error to <2% of commanded MW within 200 ms for FCR-grade service | 9 | 6 | 12 | reliability | T (A1) | A1 |
| 10 | EXECUTE | Execute | Overseer | ROBUSTNESS | Minimize the likelihood of Li-ion thermal runaway or fire event over the asset lifetime (target <1e-6 per asset-year) | 10 | 6 | 14 | safety | T (A1) | A1 |
| 11 | EXECUTE | Execute | Executor | ROBUSTNESS | Minimize the likelihood of simultaneous unavailability during a redispatch event (SoC too low or too high to serve DSO command) | 9 | 4 | 14 | reliability | T (A1) | A1 |
| 12 | EXECUTE | Execute | Beneficiary | ACCURACY | Minimize voltage-band violations (>±10% Un) at the coupling point during storage dispatch | 9 | 7 | 11 | reliability | F | — |
| 13 | EXECUTE | Execute | Executor | EFFORT | Minimize the number of manual operator interventions required per 100 dispatch events (target <2) | 7 | 5 | 9 | skill | F | — |
| 14 | EXECUTE | Execute | Overseer | ROBUSTNESS | Minimize the likelihood of IEC 62443 / BSI-KRITIS cyber incidents affecting the storage asset (target zero utility-impacting events) | 10 | 5 | 15 | safety | F | — |
| 15 | MONITOR | Monitor | Executor | DETECTION | Minimize mean time to detect battery capacity fade >3% per year beyond warranty curve (target <30 days) | 8 | 4 | 12 | reliability | T (A1) | A1 |
| 16 | MONITOR | Monitor | Executor | DETECTION | Minimize the number of undetected BMS/PCS faults reaching the DSO SCADA as clean signals (false-OK rate <0.1%) | 9 | 5 | 13 | safety | T (A1) | A1 |
| 17 | MONITOR | Monitor | Planner | ACCURACY | Increase the ability to forecast residual useful life (RUL) within ±6 months at the 5-yr mark | 7 | 3 | 11 | reliability | T (A1) | A1 |
| 18 | MONITOR | Modify | Executor | RECOVERY | Minimize mean time to restore full dispatch capability after a single PCS/module fault (target <8 h) | 8 | 5 | 11 | reliability | T (A1) | A1 |
| 19 | MONITOR | Modify | Lifecycle | EFFORT | Minimize the skill level required for first-line field service (target: trained PV/MV-EPC tech, no cell-chemistry specialist on site) | 7 | 4 | 10 | skill | T (A1) | A1 |
| 20 | DEFINE | Define | Purchase Exec | CONSISTENCY | Minimize €/kWh-installed variance across DSO tender lots of similar scope (target σ/μ <10%) | 7 | 5 | 9 | cost | F | — |
| 21 | EXECUTE | Execute | Overseer | ACCURACY | Minimize the likelihood of round-trip efficiency falling below 85% AC-AC after 5 years of contracted cycling | 7 | 5 | 9 | cost | T (A1) | A1 |
| 22 | EXECUTE | Execute | Overseer | ROBUSTNESS | Minimize the likelihood of second-life / end-of-life recycling compliance (EU Battery Reg., CRMA) failing and imposing retroactive cost | 7 | 4 | 10 | cost | T (A1) | A1 |

### 3.5 P3/P4/P5 bridge needs (hypothesis)

| Lvl | Role | Retrieval → Bridge → Need |
|---|---|---|
| P3 Role | DSO Planner | "Become the planner who deferred reinforcement without an outage" → BESS must demonstrably replace one substation on the 10-yr plan |
| P4 Image | DSO CTO | "Be seen by BNetzA as a digital/innovative Netzbetreiber" → Asset must be showcased in regulator-facing forums; public dashboards |
| P5 Emotion | Grid Control Operator | "Avoid the feeling of unpredictable asset behavior at 3am" → Deterministic, boring, SCADA-clean operation |

All P3–P5 marked `[hypothesis]` until operator interviews.

### 3.6 Needs mix (QA)

| Metric | Target | Actual |
|---|---|---|
| Total error statements | 15–30 | **22** ✅ |
| ≥2 needs per relevant step | ≥2 | DEFINE 4, PREPARE 4, EXECUTE 10, MONITOR 4 ✅ |
| Market-inherent (PR=F) | 60–70% | 9 / 22 = **41%** ⚠ (BESS-heavy skew reflects Li-ion-specific failure modes) |
| Technology-specific (PR=T) | 30–40% | 13 / 22 = **59%** ⚠ |
| "Minimize" vs "Increase" | ~60/40 | 19 Min / 3 Inc = 86/14 ⚠ |
| Solution-agnostic phrasing | 100% | PASS — no "Mein Solar", no "Fluence", no "lithium" in need text (Li-ion thermal runaway = stated physical mechanism, acceptable per Step 06) |
| Anchor level match | L5 | PASS |
| Parent referenced in Product Job | yes | PASS (L6f Flex & Congestion) |

**Underserved needs (I>7 AND S<5):** #1, #2, #4, #6, #8, #10, #11, #14, #15, #16, #18 — **11 of 22 (50%)**. Strongest cluster: PREPARE (cert + permit) + EXECUTE (availability + safety + cyber) + MONITOR (fault detection).

**Overserved (I<3 AND S>7):** none.

---

sections.title: Incumbent Anchors (for downstream feature-fit)
sections.content:
| Technology | Anchor L5 | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Fluence Gridstack / Smartstack | Grid-Scale BESS | Dominant tier-1 incumbent, ~30% share DE |
| Tesla Megapack | Grid-Scale BESS | Tier-1, 15% share, closed EMS (Autobidder) |
| Sungrow PowerTitan | Grid-Scale BESS | 12% share, liquid-cooled, aggressive €/kWh |
| Wärtsilä GridSolv | Grid-Scale BESS | 8%, strong grid-forming |
| Hitachi Energy e-mesh | Grid-Scale BESS | 5%, utility-procurement friendly |
| ABB / Siemens STATCOM | Mech. FACTS L5 | Substitute — reactive only |
| Next Kraftwerke / Entrix VPP | VPP L5 | Substitute — distributed, unreliable availability |

---

sections.title: Sources
sections.content:
- [Germany to Launch Inertia Service Market in 2026 — Herbert Smith Freehills Kramer](https://www.hsfkramer.com/notes/energy/2025-posts/germany-to-launch-inertia-service-market-in-2026-new-revenue-stream-for-grid-forming-bess)
- [German large-scale BESS — FCA, grid and revenue challenges — Energy-Storage.news](https://www.energy-storage.news/german-large-scale-bess-projects-have-built-industry-confidence-but-fca-grid-and-revenue-challenges-remain/)
- [Europe BESS Market 2026: Grid Boosters, Netzbooster & Congestion Management — NextG Power](https://nextgpower.com/europe-bess-market-2026-grid-boosters-netzbooster-congestion-management/)
- [VDE-AR-N 4110 — CUBE CONCEPTS](https://cubeconcepts.de/en/vde-ar-n-4110-connection-rules-for-medium-voltage-pv-systems/)
- [Utility-Scale Energy Storage Procurements 2026 — Morgan Lewis](https://www.morganlewis.com/pubs/2026/03/utility-scale-energy-storage-procurements-in-2026-contracting-and-risk-allocation)
- [Real-World Diagnostics and Prognostics for Grid-Connected BESS — IEEE Spectrum](https://spectrum.ieee.org/sheffield-battery-energy-storage-system-research)
- [Battery Storage Is Reshaping the Grid — POWER Magazine](https://www.powermag.com/battery-storage-is-reshaping-the-grid-integration-strategy-will-shape-the-outcome/)
- [Fluence–TransnetBW Kupferzell Grid Booster — Fluence IR](https://ir.fluenceenergy.com/news-releases/news-release-details/pioneers-energy-security-fluence-and-transnetbw-start)
- [EDF wins decentralized grid-booster battery project Germany — ess-news](https://www.ess-news.com/2025/12/15/edf-wins-decentralized-dual-use-grid-booster-battery-project-in-germany/)

rows.Value: Deliver bulk electrical energy from transmission interface to end-use premises at regulated voltage, frequency, reliability, and price
Delivered kWh (MV/LV), connection services, flexibility/congestion relief, metering data

rows.Circumstance: Long feeders, voltage drop, DG backflow

rows.Value: **PROCESS_VN** (BESS makes the CFJ happen; not inside delivered kWh)
Dispatch flexibility/storage to relieve MV/LV constraints
DSO grid-control + planning functions (acting for end-use premises)
"What dispatchable-flexibility capability does the DSO need to relieve feeder/interface constraints without deferring reinforcement beyond regulated reliability?"

rows.Alternative: **Containerized BESS EPC (incumbent — Fluence / Tesla / Sungrow / Mein Solar class)**
rows.Inherent trade-offs: Li-ion fire risk, 10-yr cal life, €250–400/kWh, 6–12 mo cert lead
No energy storage (reactive only); can't defer kWh reinforcement
Unreliable availability, depends on 3rd-party load response, no grid-forming
Zero CAPEX; mounting §13/§14a cost, AEE compensation exposure, reputational

rows.DSO-side persona: Grid control room operator; flex-dispatch desk
Head of grid planning; CTO; BNetzA (regulator)
EPC installer, TÜV/FGH certifier, O&M tech, recycler
BNetzA, VDE FNN, consulting (DNV, Lazard), insurer

rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of under-sizing BESS power/energy rating such that ≥5% of required annual constraint-hours go unserved
Increase the ability to map a specific MV feeder constraint to a ≥95%-accurate required kW/kWh dispatch profile over a 10-yr horizon
Minimize the time to produce a BNetzA/anreizregulierung-compliant business case linking reinforcement deferral to the storage asset (target <90 days)
Minimize the likelihood of grid-code certification (VDE-AR-N 4110 Type B, FGW TR8) slipping the project critical path by >3 months
Minimize the EPC interface count (number of separate counterparties the DSO must manage) to ≤1 for the storage asset
Minimize the likelihood of fire-protection/Baugenehmigung rejection post-order causing >€200k rework
Minimize variability in commissioning duration across sites with similar MV profiles (target σ <10 days across ≥5 sites)
Maximize contracted-availability hours per year delivered to the DSO dispatch signal (target ≥98% availability, ≥6,000 full-cycle equivalents over 10 yr)
Minimize dispatch setpoint-to-response error to <2% of commanded MW within 200 ms for FCR-grade service
Minimize the likelihood of Li-ion thermal runaway or fire event over the asset lifetime (target <1e-6 per asset-year)
Minimize the likelihood of simultaneous unavailability during a redispatch event (SoC too low or too high to serve DSO command)
Minimize voltage-band violations (>±10% Un) at the coupling point during storage dispatch
Minimize the number of manual operator interventions required per 100 dispatch events (target <2)
Minimize the likelihood of IEC 62443 / BSI-KRITIS cyber incidents affecting the storage asset (target zero utility-impacting events)
Minimize mean time to detect battery capacity fade >3% per year beyond warranty curve (target <30 days)
Minimize the number of undetected BMS/PCS faults reaching the DSO SCADA as clean signals (false-OK rate <0.1%)
Increase the ability to forecast residual useful life (RUL) within ±6 months at the 5-yr mark
Minimize mean time to restore full dispatch capability after a single PCS/module fault (target <8 h)
Minimize the skill level required for first-line field service (target: trained PV/MV-EPC tech, no cell-chemistry specialist on site)
Minimize €/kWh-installed variance across DSO tender lots of similar scope (target σ/μ <10%)
Minimize the likelihood of round-trip efficiency falling below 85% AC-AC after 5 years of contracted cycling
Minimize the likelihood of second-life / end-of-life recycling compliance (EU Battery Reg., CRMA) failing and imposing retroactive cost

rows.Retrieval → Bridge → Need: "Become the planner who deferred reinforcement without an outage" → BESS must demonstrably replace one substation on the 10-yr plan
"Be seen by BNetzA as a digital/innovative Netzbetreiber" → Asset must be showcased in regulator-facing forums; public dashboards
"Avoid the feeling of unpredictable asset behavior at 3am" → Deterministic, boring, SCADA-clean operation

rows.Actual: PASS — no "Mein Solar", no "Fluence", no "lithium" in need text (Li-ion thermal runaway = stated physical mechanism, acceptable per Step 06)

entities.cfj_industry: Deliver bulk electrical energy from the transmission interface to end-use premises at regulated voltage, frequency, reliability, and price

anchor.rationale: BESS is equipment the DSO uses to execute the L6f job family. Not part of delivered kWh. Product abstraction = standalone system → L5.

lens.function_verb: Dispatch flexibility/storage to relieve MV/LV constraints
lens.lens_statement: What dispatchable-flexibility capability does the DSO need to relieve feeder/interface constraints without deferring reinforcement beyond regulated reliability?

entities.core_job: Relieve a defined MV/LV grid constraint on demand, without violating voltage/frequency/reliability obligations or exceeding the reinforcement-deferral cost ceiling
entities.product_job_statement: Help the DSO Flexibility & Congestion Management function to inject or absorb multi-MW real and reactive power at a specified grid node, on sub-second notice, under VDE-AR-N 4110 compliance, for a contracted lifetime and availability
entities.abstraction_test: PASS — alternatives span 5 categories (BESS, STATCOM, VPP, copper reinforcement, curtailment)

segments.name: Rural radial feeders
segments.circumstance: Long feeders, voltage drop, DG backflow

stakeholders.persona: Head of grid planning; CTO; BNetzA
EPC installer; TÜV/FGH certifier; O&M tech; recycler
BNetzA; VDE FNN; consulting (DNV, Lazard); insurer

job_map.relevance_rationale: DEFINE = tender/spec sizing; PREPARE = install + cert; EXECUTE = dispatch (core); MONITOR = SoC + fault telemetry. LOCATE/CONFIRM/MODIFY collapsed into adjacent steps. CONCLUDE deferred (decommission >10 yr out).

error_statements.statement: Minimize the likelihood of under-sizing BESS power/energy rating such that ≥5% of required annual constraint-hours go unserved
Increase the ability to map a specific MV feeder constraint to a ≥95%-accurate required kW/kWh dispatch profile over a 10-yr horizon
Minimize the time to produce a BNetzA/anreizregulierung-compliant business case linking reinforcement deferral to the storage asset (target <90 days)
Minimize the likelihood of grid-code certification (VDE-AR-N 4110 Type B, FGW TR8) slipping the project critical path by >3 months
Minimize the EPC interface count (number of separate counterparties the DSO must manage) to ≤1 for the storage asset
Minimize the likelihood of fire-protection or Baugenehmigung rejection post-order causing >€200k rework
Minimize variability in commissioning duration across sites with similar MV profiles (target σ <10 days across ≥5 sites)
Maximize contracted-availability hours per year delivered to the DSO dispatch signal (target ≥98% availability, ≥6,000 full-cycle equivalents over 10 yr)
Minimize dispatch setpoint-to-response error to <2% of commanded MW within 200 ms for FCR-grade service
Minimize the likelihood of Li-ion thermal runaway or fire event over the asset lifetime (target <1e-6 per asset-year)
Minimize the likelihood of simultaneous unavailability during a redispatch event (SoC too low or too high to serve DSO command)
Minimize voltage-band violations (>±10% Un) at the coupling point during storage dispatch
Minimize the number of manual operator interventions required per 100 dispatch events (target <2)
Minimize the likelihood of IEC 62443 or BSI-KRITIS cyber incidents affecting the storage asset (target zero utility-impacting events)
Minimize mean time to detect battery capacity fade >3% per year beyond warranty curve (target <30 days)
Minimize the number of undetected BMS or PCS faults reaching the DSO SCADA as clean signals (false-OK rate <0.1%)
Increase the ability to forecast residual useful life within ±6 months at the 5-yr mark
Minimize mean time to restore full dispatch capability after a single PCS or module fault (target <8 h)
Minimize the skill level required for first-line field service (target: trained PV/MV-EPC technician, no cell-chemistry specialist required on site)
Minimize €/kWh-installed variance across DSO tender lots of similar scope (target σ/μ <10%)
Minimize the likelihood of round-trip efficiency falling below 85% AC-AC after 5 years of contracted cycling
Minimize the likelihood of end-of-life recycling compliance (EU Battery Reg., CRMA) failing and imposing retroactive cost on the DSO
